intent: The callings of the village — open-ended desires that give agents purpose.

interpretation.this_is: Callings are not tasks. They are the village's way of saying 'this matters to me.'

callings.name: The Village Needs Safety
callings.description: The village feels exposed. It wants walls, watchtowers, and a place to shelter.
callings.story: The village remembers the night the wolves howled outside.

callings.name: The Village Needs a Heart
callings.description: The village wants a place to gather, share, and decide together.
callings.story: The village gathered under the old oak tree to decide what to build next.

callings.name: The Village Wants to See Beyond
callings.description: The village is curious about what lies beyond the forest, the hills, the river.
responses: Send an expedition to explore the hills.
Build a watchtower to see further.
callings.story: Terra saw a strange light on the eastern mountain and the village wondered what it was.

callings.name: The Village Wants to Be Beautiful
callings.description: The village wants flowers, fountains, art. It wants to be a place worth remembering.
callings.story: The village believes that beauty is not a luxury — it is a memory.

callings.name: The Village Wants to Remember
callings.description: The village wants to record its deeds, its dead, its dreams.
callings.story: The village remembers those who came before and those who will come after.

callings.name: The Village Wants to Be Remembered
callings.description: The village wants heroes, stories, and a name that echoes across the ages.
responses: Tell the story of the village.
callings.story: The village believes that a civilization is only as great as its stories.

decision.how: Agents discuss which calling matters most. They vote. The village decides.
decision.why: The village is not a machine. It is a living thing. It chooses its own purpose.
